name: John Bonham
abstract: John Henry Bonham (31 May 1948 – 25 September 1980) was an English musician who was the drummer of the rock band Led Zeppelin. Noted for his speed, power, fast single-footed kick drumming, distinctive sound, and feel for groove, he is regarded as one of the greatest and most influential drummers in history.

works_created.t: Hey, Hey, What Can I Do

about_quotes.speaker: Elvin Jones
about_quotes.evidence: John Bonham... created his style after that... everyone is playing Elvin Jones’ rhythm, Elvin Jones’ concept of time.
about_quotes.doc_title: Elvin Jones — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
